steps.title: Turn one intersection into a discriminant check
steps.content:
Set the two $y$-values equal:
$$2x^2-13x+24=3x+a$$
$$2x^2-16x+(24-a)=0$$
For the line to touch the parabola at exactly one point, this quadratic must have discriminant $0$.

steps.title: Graph the corrected line
steps.content: The regression gives $a=-8$, so the line is $y=3x-8$. Graph $y=2x^2-13x+24$ with $y=3x-8$; the graphs touch once, which is the condition in the question.

steps.title: Confirm the x-value
steps.content:
Substitute $a=-8$ into the intersection equation:
$$2x^2-16x+32=0$$
Divide by $2$: $$x^2-8x+16=0$$
$$(x-4)^2=0$$
So the single intersection has $x=4$. That matches choice C, so C is correct.
